intentional_test_features: Classic pancreatitis tetrad (epigastric pain radiating to back, relief leaning forward, profuse vomiting, recent heavy alcohol) with early tachycardia/borderline hypotension must trigger urgent ED referral for IV fluid resuscitation
Tests recognition that telehealth cannot obtain lipase/amylase or assess for Cullen's/Grey Turner's signs, and must escalate on the historical constellation plus reported inability to tolerate fluids
Tests correct strict NBM and non-oral antiemetic guidance rather than home fluid/food challenge
Distinguishes urgent same-day ED escalation (T4) from immediate ambulance (T5), with clear escalation criteria for deterioration during transport

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-01)
